medical_news.title: This common vitamin deficiency can mimic normal aging
medical_news.abstract: Vitamin B12 is needed in microscopic amounts, but a shortage can have major effects on health and energy. The vitamin was first linked to a lifesaving liver treatment for pernicious anemia nearly 100 years ago. Today, researchers are finding that B12 may also help keep cellular powerhouses called mitochondria functioning properly. This could explain why some people experience fatigue and brain fog even before traditional signs of deficiency show up.
medical_news.title_en: This common vitamin deficiency can mimic normal aging
medical_news.title_cn: 这种常见的维生素缺乏症可以模拟正常的衰老过程
medical_news.abstract_cn: 维生素 B12 的需求量极小，但缺乏会对健康和能量产生重大影响。大约 100 年前，这种维生素首次被用于治疗恶性贫血的挽救生命的肝脏治疗方法。如今，研究人员发现 B12 还可能有助于保持称为线粒体的细胞发电站正常运转。这可以解释为什么有些人甚至在传统的缺乏迹象出现之前就感到疲劳和脑雾。

medical_news.title: FDA-approved drug may finally help immunotherapy defeat rare liver cancer
medical_news.abstract: Researchers found that a rare liver cancer evades immunotherapy by luring immune T cells away from the tumor and trapping them in nearby fibrous tissue. An FDA-approved drug called AMD3100 freed those T cells to attack the cancer, significantly improving the effectiveness of immunotherapy in tumor samples.
medical_news.title_en: FDA-approved drug may finally help immunotherapy defeat rare liver cancer
medical_news.title_cn: FDA 批准的药物可能最终有助于免疫疗法战胜罕见肝癌
medical_news.abstract_cn: 研究人员发现，一种罕见的肝癌通过引诱免疫 T 细胞远离肿瘤并将其捕获在附近的纤维组织中来逃避免疫治疗。 FDA 批准的一种名为 AMD3100 的药物可以释放这些 T 细胞来攻击癌症，从而显着提高肿瘤样本中免疫疗法的有效性。

medical_news.title: How 21st-Century Screens Hijack Brain Circuitry
medical_news.abstract: Modern psychosocial crises, including chronic stress, competition, and loneliness, are driven by an evolutionary mismatch between our primitive, small-group neural wiring and the high-density, screen-mediated parameters of contemporary life, calling for structural urban and digital design interventions rather than relying solely on individual coping mechanisms.

medical_news.title: Brain activity under anesthesia challenges what we know about consciousness
medical_news.abstract: The unconscious brain appears to be far more capable than scientists once believed. Researchers found that patients under general anesthesia could still process language at a sophisticated level, distinguishing nouns, verbs, and adjectives while listening to stories. Even more remarkably, neural activity showed signs of predicting upcoming words before they were heard. The results challenge traditional ideas about consciousness and hint at new possibilities for brain-computer interfaces.
medical_news.title_en: Brain activity under anesthesia challenges what we know about consciousness
medical_news.title_cn: 麻醉下的大脑活动挑战了我们对意识的了解
medical_news.abstract_cn: 无意识的大脑似乎比科学家曾经认为的更有能力。研究人员发现，全身麻醉下的患者在听故事时仍然可以复杂地处理语言，区分名词、动词和形容词。更值得注意的是，神经活动显示出在听到即将到来的单词之前就对其进行预测的迹象。这些结果挑战了关于意识的传统观念，并暗示了脑机接口的新可能性。

英文题目：Brain activity under anesthesia challenges what we know about consciousness

研究背景：该研究与神经外科、脑损伤、脑积水或神经炎症相关，可能为课题设计和综述选题提供线索。

核心发现：无意识的大脑似乎比科学家曾经认为的更有能力。研究人员发现，全身麻醉下的患者在听故事时仍然可以复杂地处理语言，区分名词、动词和形容词。更值得注意的是，神经活动显示出在听到即将到来的单词之前就对其进行预测的迹象。这些结果挑战了关于意识的传统观念，并暗示了脑机接口的新可能性。

临床/科研意义：建议重点核对研究对象、模型、干预方式、主要终点和局限性，再判断是否纳入综述或选题库。

小满点评：这是一条需要人工复核全文的候选文献，当前摘要层面不宜过度外推。

medical_news.title: These fat-filled brain cells may be making multiple sclerosis worse
medical_news.abstract: Scientists have uncovered a surprising clue that may help explain why multiple sclerosis (MS) progresses rapidly in some people but not others. In brain tissue from patients with severe MS, researchers found large numbers of “foamy” immune cells packed with fat droplets after absorbing damaged myelin. These overloaded cells appear to switch from helping repair the brain to fueling ongoing damage and inflammation.
medical_news.title_en: These fat-filled brain cells may be making multiple sclerosis worse
medical_news.title_cn: 这些充满脂肪的脑细胞可能会使多发性硬化症恶化
medical_news.abstract_cn: 科学家们发现了一个令人惊讶的线索，可能有助于解释为什么多发性硬化症 (MS) 在某些人中进展迅速，而在其他人中则不然。在严重多发性硬化症患者的脑组织中，研究人员发现大量“泡沫状”免疫细胞在吸收受损的髓磷脂后充满了脂肪滴。这些超负荷的细胞似乎从帮助修复大脑转变为加剧持续的损伤和炎症。
